name: Mardi Gras Prize King's Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg, lightly beaten with 1 tbsp milk
Dime, dried bean or miniature doll
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften yeast in warm water.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, nutmeg, salt, and lemon rind.
3. Make a well in the center and add the yeast mixture, milk, eggs, egg yolks, and mix thoroughly.
4. Add butter until the dough forms a ball. Place on a floured board and knead until smooth and elastic.
5. Turn the dough into a buttered bowl, cover with a towel, and let rise for 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk.
6. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
7. Brush the baking sheet with butter.
8. Punch down the dough on a floured board, knead, then pat into a 14-inch cylinder.
9. Place on the prepared baking sheet and form into a large ring.
10. Press the trinket into the dough so it is hidden. Cover and let rise for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
11. Before baking, brush the top with the egg milk mixture.
12. Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack.
13. Beat the icing ingredients until smooth.
14. Spread over the top of the cake, letting it drip down the sides.
15. Immediately sprinkle the sugars in wide strips (purple, green, and yellow) over the icing.
